title: Understanding the Problem
text: To find the time it takes for the ball to reach the green, we need to consider the vertical motion of the ball. The ball is dropped from a height of $$400$$ $$m$$. We can use the kinematic equation for vertical motion to find the time.

title: Kinematic Equation for Vertical Motion
text:
The kinematic equation for vertical motion is:

$$h$$ $$=$$ $$v_0 t$$ + $$0.5g t^2$$

where:
- $$h$$ is the height (400 m)
- $$v_0$$ is the initial vertical velocity (0 $$ rac{m}{s}$$, since the ball is dropped)
- g is the acceleration due to gravity $$(9.8$$ m/s**2)
- $$t$$ is the time in seconds

title: Solving for Time
text:
Rearrange the kinematic equation to solve for time (t):

$$0$$ $$=$$ $$0.5g t^2$$ - $$h$$

Solve for $$t$$.
